etymology.description: Phonetic 爻 + phonetic 六 -> 𦥑 added -> semantic 子 (child) added for differentiation -> 六 corrupted to 冖 (cover) and 𦥑 combined with 冖 -> 學
etymology.interpretation: 學 (xué, 'learn') depicts a stylized form where originally two phonetic components, 爻 and 六, were combined. A semantic 子 (zǐ, 'child') was later added to suggest learning activities typically associated with children. 爻 represents a concept of complexity or mixing, which was relevant in the evolution of the character's meaning. Over time, 六 transformed to 冖 (a covering element) aligning with the top part of the character, leading to the current form, emphasizing the idea of learning as something collected and overseen, akin to a child learning within the confines of a structured environment.
etymology.simplification: In simplified form, 学 takes a different route, reducing 學 by modifying the top components and removing 子 for ease of writing.

examples.chinese: 她每天都努力學習(她每天都努力學習)。
我在學校里交了很多朋友(我在學校裏交了很多朋友)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zài xuéxiào lǐ jiāo le hěn duō péngyou.
examples.english: I made many friends at school.

examples.chinese: 他想學中文(他想學中文)，所以他報名了一個課程(所以他報名了一個課程)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xiǎng xué Zhōngwén, suǒyǐ tā bàomíng le yī gè kèchéng.
examples.english: He wants to learn Chinese, so he enrolled in a course.
